title: February 2022 — nursery-homes, dispensaries, pop-up shops, and a Smash mortality metaphor
html:
TL;DR

  • Six-line February 2022 brainstorm pairing real-estate / consumer business ideas (nursery homes, dispensaries, pop-up shops, cost analysis) with a Smash Bros. mortality metaphor — "died in smash without realizing it, on our final life."
  • "Nursery homes" likely a typo for nursing homes — placing this alongside 2022 09 13 drive thru weed and 2022 10 05 potential business ideas in the young yahir business impulse sweep.
  • "Dispensaries" is Yahir's recurring 2022 cannabis-retail thesis — also surfaces in 2022 09 13 drive thru weed (drive-thru cash-flow angle).
  • The "mint eater vs junk-food eater" line is a type chart-flavored Pokémon/Smash-style metaphor for personality — Yahir thinking in game-mechanics frames at age 19.
  • The smash as life metaphor line ("died without realizing it, final life") is a quiet memento mori — same philosophical bent as the 2022 11 05 shot dream numb dream.

What's actually in the note

-dispensaries
-pop up shops
-figure out the costs regarding our items
-died in smash without realizing it, and were on our final life — Either a mint eater or a junk food eater
(title: nursery homes)

Why it matters

Two threads cross here:

  1. Business-ideas log. Nursing homes + dispensaries + pop-up shops is a coherent "boring local-services arbitrage" thesis — a younger version of the bridge income strategies frame. The "we" pronoun suggests a co-thinker, possibly jackson or Tio Edgar.
  2. Smash as ontology. The "final life" line uses Smash mechanics as a death-awareness frame. This is the first in-vault use of game-mechanics-as-life-metaphor — predates the type effectiveness chart concept page by two years.

Open questions

  • Who is the "we"? (Most likely jackson given 2022 collaboration density; possibly Tio Edgar.)
  • "Mint eater vs junk-food eater" — was this a real classification system or a joke? (Reads as personality-archetype shorthand; could become a concept if it recurs.)

Wikilinks
